market.summary: The global art market is experiencing a period of 'modest growth' and 'structural recalibration' in March 2026. Total art sales globally rose by 4% year-on-year in 2025 to $59.6 billion, driven by high-end sales and U.S. dominance (44% global share).
keyTrends: Strong recovery in high-value works (>$10M segment)
Shift back to in-person art fairs (35% of dealer turnover)
Restructuring around local collectors with selectivity in lower-tier acquisitions
Gender representation improving: 50% of artists at primary-market galleries

highlights: 2026 outlook: Cautious optimism among dealers (43% expecting improvement)
Auction activity in London strong with Modern and Contemporary sales
Old Masters sector seeing notable jump (30% increase in 2025)
Technological innovation and emerging collector markets as key 2026 drivers
